description: Elixir/OTP subsystems under Esr.Runtime: AdapterHub (adapter-topic↔actor binding), HandlerRouter (Phoenix-channel dispatch to Python handler workers), Topology Instantiator/Registry (YAML artifact → running PeerServers), ETS-backed Persistence with disk checkpointing, Telemetry buffer+attach, and the Workspaces registry cache (PRD 01 F08/F11/F13/F14/F15/F16/F18, spec §3.1/3.3/3.4/3.5/3.6).

key_symbols.name: Esr.AdapterHub.Registry
key_symbols.description: ETS-backed map topic → actor_id for adapter Phoenix topics `adapter:<name>/<instance_id>`. Table `:esr_adapter_hub_bindings`; public reads skip the GenServer (ETS public/set).

key_symbols.name: bind/2
key_symbols.description: Upserts {topic, actor_id}; GenServer resolves actor_id → pid via Esr.PeerRegistry and Process.monitor's it so DOWN cleans the binding.

key_symbols.name: unbind/1
key_symbols.description: Idempotent delete of topic + its monitor bookkeeping.

key_symbols.name: lookup/1
key_symbols.description: O(1) ETS read → {:ok, actor_id} | :error.

key_symbols.name: list/0
key_symbols.description: Full table dump for admin/GC.

key_symbols.name: handle_info/:DOWN
key_symbols.description: Reviewer S5 ghost-entry prevention: when a monitored PeerServer pid dies every topic bound to it is removed synchronously.

key_symbols.name: Esr.AdapterHub.Supervisor
key_symbols.description: one_for_one supervising [Esr.AdapterHub.Registry]; future adapter session workers live here (F08+).

key_symbols.name: Esr.HandlerRouter
key_symbols.description: PeerServer→handler-worker call/reply over Phoenix PubSub. v0.1 uses worker_id='default' (F10 pool pending).

key_symbols.name: call/3
key_symbols.description: Subscribe caller to handler_reply:<id>, broadcast handler_call envelope on handler:<module>/default, await matching {:handler_reply, env} or 5s timeout.

key_symbols.name: shape_reply/1
key_symbols.description: {:ok, new_state, actions} | {:error, {:handler_error, err}} | {:error, {:handler_error, {type:'InvalidReply'}}}.

key_symbols.name: Esr.HandlerRouter.Supervisor
key_symbols.description: Empty scaffold; per-module worker pool arrives in F10.

key_symbols.name: Esr.Persistence.Ets
key_symbols.description: ETS actor-state store (table `:esr_actor_states`) with atomic tmp-rename disk checkpoint for G-4 kill-9 recovery.

key_symbols.name: put/3 / get/2 / delete/2 / clear/1
key_symbols.description: Direct ETS CRUD keyed by actor_id; public table, no round-trip through GenServer.

key_symbols.name: save_to_disk/2
key_symbols.description: tab2list → :erlang.term_to_binary → write <path>.tmp → File.rename (atomic).

key_symbols.name: load_from_disk/2
key_symbols.description: binary_to_term re-populates the ETS table; missing file → {:ok, 0}.

key_symbols.name: Esr.Persistence.Supervisor
key_symbols.description: Starts Ets child with table `:esr_actor_states` (PeerServer read/write target).

key_symbols.name: Esr.Telemetry.Attach
key_symbols.description: Attaches a :telemetry.attach_many handler for 20 bounded `[:esr, _, _]` events (no wildcard because :telemetry disallows it).

key_symbols.name: @events
key_symbols.description: Enumeration: actor spawned/stopped/paused/resumed; message received/dispatched; directive issued/completed/timeout; handler called/violation/timeout/crashed/retry_exhausted; topology activated/deactivated; adapter crashed; deadletter event; state oversized_warning; ipc source_mismatch.

key_symbols.name: attach/1
key_symbols.description: Idempotent (detaches prior handler with same id) register against the named Buffer.

key_symbols.name: handle/4
key_symbols.description: Forwards into Esr.Telemetry.Buffer.record/4.

key_symbols.name: Esr.Telemetry.Buffer
key_symbols.description: ETS :ordered_set ring keyed by {ts_unix_ms, make_ref()}; per-name table :esr_telemetry_<name>; default retention 15 minutes.

key_symbols.name: Buffer.Event
key_symbols.description: {ts_unix_ms, event, measurements, metadata}.

key_symbols.name: record/4
key_symbols.description: Direct ETS insert (no GenServer hop) so every :telemetry fire is O(1).

key_symbols.name: query/2
key_symbols.description: ets.select with ms ≥ now - duration_seconds*1000; default 900s window.

key_symbols.name: handle_info/:prune
key_symbols.description: Periodic select_delete of entries older than retention_minutes; self-reschedules.

key_symbols.name: Esr.Telemetry.Supervisor
key_symbols.description: Children: default Buffer (name :default) + transient Task that runs Attach.attach(:default) once Buffer is up.

key_symbols.name: Esr.Topology.Instantiator
key_symbols.description: YAML-artifact → live actors pipeline (PRD 01 F13). Idempotent on (name, params).

key_symbols.name: instantiate/3
key_symbols.description: Entry point; short-circuits via TopoRegistry.lookup when (name, params) already live.

key_symbols.name: do_instantiate/4
key_symbols.description: check_params → validate_workspace_apps → substitute_all → toposort → spawn_in_order → TopoRegistry.register → emit [:esr, :topology, :instantiated].

key_symbols.name: validate_workspace_apps/1
key_symbols.description: v0.2 §3.3: if workspace param set, its chats' app_ids must all match an adapter binding `adapter:feishu/feishu-app:<app_id>`.

key_symbols.name: check_params/2
key_symbols.description: Every required artifact param must be present in params map.

key_symbols.name: substitute_string/2
key_symbols.description: Regex replaces {{name}}; raises ArgumentError (no String.to_atom) when a template param is missing (reviewer S6).

key_symbols.name: toposort/1 + kahn/5
key_symbols.description: Kahn's algorithm over depends_on; returns {:error, :cycle_in_depends_on} when unresolved.

key_symbols.name: spawn_in_order/3 + spawn_loop/4
key_symbols.description: For each id in topo order: start_peer → bind_adapter → ensure_python_workers → issue_init_directive; any init-directive failure triggers rollback_spawned.

key_symbols.name: issue_init_directive/2
key_symbols.description: Sends a 'directive' envelope on adapter:<a>/<id>; waits for {:directive_ack, %{id, payload.ok: true}}; re-broadcasts every 1s until deadline to handle late adapter joins.

key_symbols.name: rollback_spawned/2
key_symbols.description: Stops previously spawned peers AND synchronously unbinds their adapter topics so a retry doesn't observe stale bindings.

key_symbols.name: ensure_python_workers/1
key_symbols.description: Idempotent: WorkerSupervisor.ensure_handler + ensure_adapter with config loaded from ~/.esrd/*/adapters.yaml.

key_symbols.name: Esr.Topology.Registry
key_symbols.description: Two ETS tables: :esr_topology_handles (live handles) + :esr_topology_artifacts (compiled YAML artifacts by name).

key_symbols.name: Registry.Handle
key_symbols.description: {name, params, peer_ids} — canonical identity of a live topology.

key_symbols.name: register/3
key_symbols.description: Atomic ets.insert_new keyed by (name, sort(params)); duplicate → return existing (reviewer S7 no-TOCTOU).

key_symbols.name: lookup/2
key_symbols.description: Params-order-independent lookup via canonical key.

key_symbols.name: put_artifact/2, get_artifact/1
key_symbols.description: Compiled-topology cache used by PeerServer's InvokeCommand action dispatch.

key_symbols.name: deactivate/1
key_symbols.description: Stop cascade: stops peer_ids in REVERSE topo order, deletes handle, emits [:esr, :topology, :deactivated] (PRD 01 F14).

key_symbols.name: load_artifacts_from_dir/0
key_symbols.description: Bootstrap scan of ~/.esrd/default/commands/.compiled/*.yaml at init/1 (disabled in :test via :bootstrap_artifacts=false).

key_symbols.name: Esr.Topology.Supervisor
key_symbols.description: Children: [Esr.Topology.Registry]; Instantiator is a pure module, not a process.

key_symbols.name: Esr.Workspaces.Registry
key_symbols.description: ETS (:esr_workspaces) cache of workspaces.yaml rows loaded on esrd boot.

key_symbols.name: Workspace
key_symbols.description: {name, cwd, start_cmd, role, chats, env} — one workspace row.

key_symbols.name: get/1, list/0
key_symbols.description: Direct ETS reads (no GenServer hop).

key_symbols.name: put/1
key_symbols.description: GenServer call used by CLI push (cli:workspace/register).

key_symbols.name: load_from_file/1
key_symbols.description: YamlElixir parser; returns {name: Workspace}; missing file → {:ok, %{}}.

key_symbols.name: 7 tests (F08 Phoenix-channel layer)
key_symbols.description: Topic join; event/directive_ack routing to bound PeerServer; envelope shapes (kind=event|directive_ack); late-binding tolerance; dead-peer error path.

key_symbols.name: 8 tests
key_symbols.description: bind upsert, re-bind replace, unbind idempotency, list, auto-unbind on monitored pid DOWN (S5).

key_symbols.name: 3 tests
key_symbols.description: Happy handler_reply → {:ok, state, actions}; timeout → {:error, :handler_timeout}; error payload → {:error, {:handler_error, _}}.

key_symbols.name: 6 tests
key_symbols.description: handler:<m>/<w> join; topic format rejection; handler_reply push rebroadcast; envelope kind=handler_reply; missing id rejection; unhandled event reply.

key_symbols.name: 6 tests across 3 describes
key_symbols.description: put/get, delete, save/load round-trip, missing-file load returns {:ok, 0}.

key_symbols.name: 1 test (end-to-end)
key_symbols.description: Synthetic [:esr, :actor, :spawned] emit is observable via Buffer.query(:default, _).

key_symbols.name: 3 tests
key_symbols.description: record/query round-trip; duration_seconds window filter; empty query returns [].

key_symbols.name: 3 tests
key_symbols.description: init_directive ok ack spawns all nodes; error ack rolls back predecessors; timeout rolls back.

key_symbols.name: 7 tests
key_symbols.description: single/chain spawn; missing_params; undeclared-template raises (S6); cycle rejection; idempotency; adapter binding side-effect.

key_symbols.name: 8 tests
key_symbols.description: register key uniqueness, idempotent (name, params), different params yield new handles, lookup, list_all, deactivate removes, concurrent registers atomic (S7).

key_symbols.name: 3 tests
key_symbols.description: deactivate unregisters peers; emits :topology :deactivated telemetry; stop order is reverse of spawn order.

key_symbols.name: 2 tests
key_symbols.description: v0.2 §3.3: reject unknown workspace app_id; skip when workspace param absent.

interfaces.name: Esr.AdapterHub.Registry.{bind,unbind,lookup,list}
interfaces.description: Topic (adapter:<name>/<instance_id>) ↔ actor_id binding; auto-GCs on actor pid DOWN.

interfaces.name: Esr.HandlerRouter.call/3
interfaces.description: Synchronously dispatch a handler_call envelope to handler:<module>/default and await handler_reply (default 5s).

interfaces.name: Esr.Persistence.Ets.{put,get,delete,clear,save_to_disk,load_from_disk}
interfaces.description: Actor-state CRUD + atomic disk checkpoint (tmp+rename) + boot rehydrate (Track G-4).

interfaces.name: Esr.Telemetry.Buffer.{record,query}
interfaces.description: Append + time-window select over ETS :ordered_set; O(1) write, no GenServer hop.

interfaces.name: Esr.Telemetry.Attach.attach/1
interfaces.description: Attach bounded [:esr, _, _] handler (20 events) into a named Buffer at boot.

interfaces.name: Esr.Topology.Instantiator.instantiate/3
interfaces.description: Idempotent YAML→actors: validate → toposort → spawn+bind+init_directive → register+telemetry.

interfaces.name: Esr.Topology.Registry.{register,lookup,list_all,put_artifact,get_artifact,deactivate}
interfaces.description: Handle + artifact ETS registry; atomic insert_new; deactivate fires reverse-order stop cascade (F14).

interfaces.name: Esr.Workspaces.Registry.{get,list,put,load_from_file}
interfaces.description: In-memory workspaces.yaml cache keyed by workspace name.

dependencies: Phoenix.PubSub + Phoenix.Channel via EsrWeb.Endpoint (Handler/Adapter channels + reply pubsub)
YamlElixir (parsing workspaces.yaml, adapters.yaml, compiled command YAML)
Cross-module: Esr.PeerRegistry (bind→pid), Esr.PeerSupervisor (start/stop_peer), Esr.WorkerSupervisor (ensure_handler/adapter Python subprocesses), EsrWeb.Endpoint (broadcast)

test_results.command: cd /home/yaosh/projects/esr/runtime && MIX_ENV=test mix test test/esr/adapter_hub/ test/esr/handler_router/ test/esr/persistence/ test/esr/telemetry/ test/esr/topology/ --max-failures 0
test_results.raw_output_first_1000:
adapter_hub + handler_router group: 24 tests, 0 failures (0.9s).
persistence + telemetry group: 9 tests, 0 failures (0.5s).
topology group: 23 tests, 0 failures (3.9s).
Total: 56 tests, 0 failures across 12 files. (Note: the combined invocation above aborts with '0 tests, --max-failures reached' due to a Mix build-lock race when multiple top-level paths are compiled concurrently from a cold build cache; splitting into the three subgroups shown makes every test execute and pass. One code warning: duplicate defp issue_init_directive/2 clauses at instantiator.ex:261 and :332 — lint only, no functional impact.)

user_flows.name: AdapterHub: Python adapter subprocess registers its topic
steps: Topology.Instantiator.spawn_in_order calls bind_adapter/1 → HubRegistry.bind("adapter:<name>/<instance_id>", actor_id).
Parallel: ensure_python_workers/1 spawns Python adapter_runner which connects ws://…/adapter_hub/socket/websocket and joins the same topic via Phoenix Channel.
Inbound 'event' / 'directive_ack' from Python → channel push → AdapterHub (channel layer) looks up actor_id in Registry → forwards to PeerServer as {:inbound_event, _} / {:directive_ack, _}.
When PeerServer pid exits, the Registry's monitor fires :DOWN → all topics owned by that pid are ets.deleted so no ghost routes.

user_flows.name: HandlerRouter: PeerServer dispatches to a Python handler worker
steps: PeerServer calls Esr.HandlerRouter.call(handler_module, payload, timeout).

HandlerRouter generates id='hc-<unique>', subscribes caller to handler_reply:<id>, builds an 'envelope' %{kind: 'handler_call', id, ts, source, payload} and EsrWeb.Endpoint.broadcast on handler:<module>/default.
Python handler_worker joined on that topic filters event=='envelope' & payload.kind=='handler_call', runs the pure handler fn, pushes handler_reply envelope back.
HandlerRouter's receive block matches on id, then shape_reply/1 returns {:ok, new_state, actions} | {:error, :handler_timeout} | {:error, {:handler_error, detail}}.

user_flows.name: Topology.Instantiator: compiled YAML → live running actors
steps: Esr.Topology.Instantiator.instantiate(artifact, params) — artifact comes from Python compile_to_yaml; early-exits if TopoRegistry.lookup(name, params) already has a handle (idempotency).
check_params: every artifact['params'] key must appear in supplied params.
validate_workspace_apps: if params['workspace'] set, every chat.app_id must match an `adapter:feishu/feishu-app:<app_id>` binding in AdapterHub.
substitute_all: walk each node's id/adapter/depends_on/params/init_directive.args and Regex.replace {{name}} → params[name]; undeclared template raises ArgumentError (not String.to_atom — S6).
toposort (Kahn): reject depends_on cycles → {:error, :cycle_in_depends_on}.

spawn_in_order per id: PeerSupervisor.start_peer(actor_id, actor_type, handler_module, initial_state=node.params) → HubRegistry.bind adapter topic → WorkerSupervisor.ensure_handler/ensure_adapter (spawn Python counterparts, config from ~/.esrd/*/adapters.yaml) → issue_init_directive (broadcast directive envelope on adapter topic, await {:directive_ack, ok: true}, re-broadcast every 1s until deadline).
On any init_directive failure: rollback_spawned stops previously started peers and synchronously unbinds their topics, returns {:error, {:init_directive_failed, id, reason}}.
TopoRegistry.register(name, params, peer_ids) → emit [:esr, :topology, :instantiated] → return Handle.

user_flows.name: Topology.Registry: lifecycle of a topology instance
steps: start: Instantiator calls Registry.register(name, params, peer_ids) → atomic :ets.insert_new keyed by (name, sort(params)); duplicate returns the existing Handle (S7 no-TOCTOU).
run: PeerServer InvokeCommand actions call Registry.get_artifact(name) to resolve the compiled YAML, then Instantiator.instantiate chains another live topology.
stop_cascade: Registry.deactivate(handle) stops peer_ids in REVERSE topo order (dependents first) via PeerSupervisor.stop_peer, deletes the ETS row, emits [:esr, :topology, :deactivated].

user_flows.name: Persistence.Ets: restart-survival checkpoint cycle
steps: At boot, Persistence.Supervisor starts Ets with table :esr_actor_states (named/public/set).
Application startup calls Ets.load_from_disk(table, path) — File.read → :erlang.binary_to_term → ets.insert per {actor_id, state}; missing file = {:ok, 0}.
PeerServer put/get/delete during normal operation — all direct ETS (no GenServer hop).
Checkpoint: Ets.save_to_disk(table, path) writes <path>.tmp then File.rename (atomic) so a BEAM kill -9 never corrupts the previous snapshot (Track G-4).

user_flows.name: Telemetry: boot-time attach + event capture
steps: Esr.Telemetry.Supervisor starts Buffer name=:default (retention_minutes from :esr config, default 15) — creates :esr_telemetry_default ordered_set ETS.
Transient Task runs Attach.attach(:default) → :telemetry.attach_many for the 20 fixed [:esr, _, _] events.
Any module firing :telemetry.execute([:esr, …], measurements, metadata) hits Attach.handle/4 → Buffer.record (direct ETS insert keyed by {now_ms, ref}).
Consumers call Buffer.query(:default, duration_seconds: N) for the last N seconds; periodic :prune evicts entries older than retention_minutes.

user_flows.name: Workspaces.Registry at runtime
steps: On esrd boot, load_from_file("~/.esrd/<instance>/workspaces.yaml") parses into {name: Workspace{cwd, start_cmd, role, chats, env}} and each row is put into the :esr_workspaces ETS.
Handlers and Instantiator.validate_workspace_apps read via Workspaces.Registry.get(name) (pure ETS, no GenServer hop).
CLI `esr workspace register` pushes new rows via put/1 (GenServer call) — single-writer funnel keeps the table consistent.

notes: Subsystem one-liners — (1) AdapterHub.Registry: maps `adapter:<name>/<instance_id>` topic → actor_id in ETS, monitors each actor pid so DOWN auto-evicts its bindings (S5); Python adapter subprocess registration is indirect — the Instantiator calls bind_adapter and WorkerSupervisor.ensure_adapter in lockstep, then the Python adapter_runner joins the Phoenix channel, inbound pushes look up the bound actor_id and forward to PeerServer. (2) HandlerRouter.call: Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe on handler_reply:<id> → broadcast handler_call envelope on handler:<module>/default → receive matching :handler_reply or time out (5 s default) — v0.1 is a singleton worker per module; F10 pool adds round-robin without changing this contract. (3) Instantiator steps: check_params → validate_workspace_apps → substitute_all ({{name}} → params[name], missing raises ArgumentError — no String.to_atom DoS) → Kahn toposort (cycle → {:error, :cycle_in_depends_on}) → per-node start_peer + bind_adapter + ensure_python_workers + issue_init_directive (re-broadcast every 1 s to tolerate late adapter joins) → TopoRegistry.register (atomic insert_new, S7) → emit [:esr, :topology, :instantiated]. Any init_directive failure triggers rollback_spawned (stops peers AND synchronously unbinds topics). (4) Topology.Registry lifecycle: register is O(1) atomic ets.insert_new keyed by (name, sort(params)) — idempotent by construction; stop_cascade (deactivate) iterates peer_ids in REVERSE topo order via PeerSupervisor.stop_peer, deletes the handle row, fires :topology :deactivated telemetry. A parallel :esr_topology_artifacts ETS caches compiled YAML; it's bootstrap-loaded at init/1 from ~/.esrd/default/commands/.compiled/*.yaml (disabled via :esr :bootstrap_artifacts=false in config/test.exs so per-test expectations still hold). (5) Persistence.Ets + restoration: named/public ETS `:esr_actor_states` holds {actor_id, state}; put/get/delete bypass the GenServer for speed; save_to_disk writes tmp+rename atomically; load_from_disk is called from the Application boot to rehydrate after a BEAM kill -9 (Track G-4). (6) Telemetry attach + buffer: Attach.@events enumerates exactly 20 prefixes (no wildcard — :telemetry disallows it) covering actor spawn/stop/pause/resume, message recv/dispatch, directive issue/complete/timeout, handler called/violation/timeout/crashed/retry_exhausted, topology activated/deactivated, adapter crashed, deadletter event, state oversized_warning, ipc source_mismatch. Buffer uses an ETS :ordered_set keyed by {ts_ms, make_ref()} so inserts are O(1), queries are range scans, and the prune task select_deletes by timestamp floor. (7) Workspaces.Registry: a workspace at runtime is a struct %Workspace{name, cwd, start_cmd, role, chats, env} cached in ETS `:esr_workspaces`; `chats` is the list driving validate_workspace_apps. Known lint: instantiator.ex has two defp issue_init_directive/2 clauses at :261 and :332 separated by helper defs — functionally equivalent (catch-all vs. specific shape) but the compiler warns 'clauses with the same name and arity should be grouped together'. Build-lock quirk: running the combined 5-subdir mix test command from a cold _build can hit a 'Waiting for lock on the build directory' race that aborts with '0 tests, --max-failures reached'; running the three subgroups separately (adapter_hub+handler_router, persistence+telemetry, topology) works and yields 24+9+23=56 tests passing.
